episode_summary: With guest Daniel Sloss, the team covers Rome’s earliest written slam of Scotland and assorted Scottish curios, Nikola Tesla’s achievements and eccentric “pigeon girlfriend,” a Swedish Lord of the Rings translation that provoked feuds with Tolkien, and the near-unbeatable checkers legend Marion Tinsley and his battles with computers.

facts.fact: The first known written reference about Scotland comes from Rome and it said, "Don't go there, it's awful."
My fact is, the man responsible for harnessing the power of Niagara Falls also had a pigeon girlfriend.
My fact this week is that the first Swedish translation of "Lord of the Rings" inspired two follow-up works.
Okay, my fact this week is that in the 1950s, the Alabama Drafts Champion proposed to the twin sister of the World Drafts Champion with the aim of creating a family of drafts geniuses.
